22.reason: [swap function expects pointers to single characters, but here it's passed pointers to entire strings (char arrays). This causes undefined behavior because swap will only exchange the first characters of the two strings, not the whole strings.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [swap function expects pointers to single characters, but here it's passed pointers to entire strings (char arrays). This causes undefined behavior because swap will only exchange the first characters of the two strings, not the whole strings.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [swap function expects pointers to single characters, but *(s+i) is a char array (char*). This causes undefined behavior as it swaps only the first character of each string.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [The swap function expects two char pointers, but *(s+i) and *(s+i+1) are arrays (char[20]), which decay to char*, so this is actually okay syntactically. However, the real issue is that 'swap' swaps only the first character of each string, not the entire string. This leads to incorrect sorting.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [The swap function expects two char pointers, but here it's swapping entire strings (char arrays) by passing char* to first element. This only swaps the first character of each string, not the whole string. Should use strcpy or a proper string swap.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [The swap function expects two char pointers, but *(s+i) and *(s+i+1) are arrays (char[20]), which decay to pointers. However, the swap function swaps only the first character of each string, not the entire string. This leads to incorrect sorting.]

60.reason: [time[sum] is uninitialized and then incremented; should be set to 1 instead of incrementing from 0.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [time[sum] is uninitialized and then incremented; should be set to 1 instead of incrementing from 0.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [time[sum] is uninitialized; it should be set to 1 for a new word, not incremented from 0.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [time[sum] is uninitialized; it should be set to 1, not incremented. Since time array is initialized to zero globally/statically, this might work by accident, but logically it's wrong: it should be time[sum] = 1;]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [time[sum] is uninitialized; should be set to 1, not incremented. This leads to undefined behavior (likely garbage value + 1).]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [time[sum] is uninitialized; it should be set to 1 instead of incrementing from 0.]

37.reason: [c should be declared as int, not char, because EOF is outside the range of char. Comparing a char to EOF can cause infinite loops or incorrect behavior on systems where char is unsigned.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [c should be declared as int, not char, because EOF is outside the range of char. Comparing a char to EOF can cause infinite loops or incorrect behavior on systems where char is unsigned.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [The variable 'c' is declared as char, but fgetc returns int. When EOF (which is -1) is stored in a char, it may become indistinguishable from a valid character (e.g., 0xFF on systems where char is signed). This can cause premature termination or infinite loops.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [c should be declared as int, not char, because EOF is outside the range of char. Comparing a char to EOF can cause infinite loops or missed EOF on some systems.]

69.reason: [Loop condition assumes word array is null-terminated beyond valid entries, but word entries beyond 'sum' may contain garbage or zeroed data due to initialization. Should loop only up to 'sum'.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop condition assumes word array is null-terminated beyond valid entries, but word entries beyond 'sum' may contain garbage or zeroed data due to initialization. Should loop only up to 'sum'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.25 , reason:  [This loop assumes that all entries up to the first empty string are valid, but after sorting, empty strings (if any) might appear in the middle or beginning. Also, 'sum' is the actual count, so loop should be for(i=0; i<sum; i++). This may print garbage or stop early.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [Loop condition assumes word array is null-terminated beyond 'sum' entries, but only first 'sum' entries are valid. Should loop i from 0 to sum-1 instead. May print garbage if unused entries aren't zeroed properly.]
